back_translation: Behold, my eyes have seen all this; my ears have heard and understood it.
What you know, I also know; I am not inferior to you.
But I desire to speak to the Almighty, and I want to argue my case before God.
You, however, smear me with lies; you are all worthless physicians.
If only you would remain silent; that would be your wisdom!
Now hear my argument, and listen to the plea of my lips.
Will you speak wickedly for God? Will you speak deceitfully for Him?
Will you show Him partiality? Will you argue the case for God?
Would it be well if He examined you? Could you deceive Him as you deceive a man?
He would surely rebuke you if you secretly showed partiality.
Would not His majesty terrify you, and the dread of Him fall upon you?
Your maxims are proverbs made of ashes; your defenses are defenses made of clay.
Be silent before me, and I will speak myself; then let come upon me whatever may.
Why do I take my flesh in my teeth and put my life in my own hand?
Though He slay me, I will hope in Him; yet I will still defend my ways to His face.
Moreover, this will be my salvation, for the godless cannot come before Him.
Listen carefully to my words, and let my declaration ring in your ears.
Behold, now I have prepared my case; I know that I will be vindicated.
Who is there that will accuse me? If so, I would be silent and die.
Only two things do not do to me, then I will not hide from Your face:
Withdraw Your hand far from me, and do not let the dread of You frighten me.
Then call, and I will answer; or let me speak, and You reply to me.
How many are my iniquities and sins? Reveal to me my transgression and sin.
Why do You hide Your face and consider me Your enemy?
Would You frighten a wind-driven leaf? Would You chase after dry chaff?
For You record bitter accusations against me and make me inherit the iniquities of my youth.
You put my feet in the stocks and watch all my paths; You set a limit around the soles of my feet.
So man wastes away like something rotten, like a garment eaten by moths.
